title: Shorea trapezifolia
intro: Shorea trapezifolia, also known as the trapezifolia tree, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. The health benefits of Shorea trapezifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Shorea trapezifolia is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Shorea trapezifolia belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Dipterocarps. This family is native to Southeast Asia and is composed of approximately 700 species. Shorea trapezifolia itself is a species of tree that is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is classified as a species of the genus Shorea, which is a group of trees commonly known as the 'benguet' or 'tengkawang' trees. Shorea trapezifolia is further classified as a species of the subgenus Shorea of the genus Shorea.
uses: Shorea trapezifolia is a tropical plant species, and it is valued for its medicinal properties. The wood and bark of this plant are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. The plant's leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat wounds and skin infections. In some cultures, the resin of Shorea trapezifolia is used to treat digestive issues. Research suggests that the plant's extracts may also have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
